Flood Directive

Thematic Overview

(TO BE COMPLETED)

https://ec.europa.eu/environment/water/flood_risk/

Floods Directive

Article 3. Competent Authorities & units of Management

  • Content: Competent Authorities can be different from WFD’s, but otherwise the same. Units of Management can be different from RBMD, but otherwise the same.

  • Structure: Same reporting structure as WFD’s.

  • First report: 2010-05-26

  • Then: when required. Update if changed at MS level.

  • End of reporting period: 3 months after required.

Article 4 & 5: Preliminary Flood Risk Assessment

  • Content: For each RBMD or Unit of Management, identify the river basins and associated coastal areas at risk of flooding.

    • Maps, including river basins, sub-basins, coastal areas. Also topography and land use.

    • Past events, including flood extent and assessment of impacts

    • Areas for which potential significant flood risks exist or might be considered likely to occur.

  • First report: 2011-12-22

  • Review: 2018-12-22

  • Then: every 6 years

  • End of reporting period: 3 months after required.

Article 6: Flood hazard maps and flood risk maps

Content:

  • Flood Hazard Maps Events:

    1. floods with a low probability, or extreme event scenarios;

    2. floods with a medium probability (likely return period ≥ 100 years);

    3. floods with a high probability, where appropriate.

    Data:

    1. the flood extent;

    2. water depths or water level, as appropriate;

    3. where appropriate, the flow velocity or the relevant water flow.

Flood Risk Maps

the indicative number of inhabitants potentially affected; 

type of economic activity of the area potentially affected; 

IPPC installations, as referred to in Annex I to Council Directive 96/61/EC of 24 September 1996 concerning integrated pollution     prevention and control) which might cause accidental pollution in case of flooding and WFD Protected Areas for: 

    Drinking Water, 

    recreational use including Bathing Waters 

    areas designated for the protection of habitats or species, including Natura 2000 sites.

  • First report: 2013-12-22

  • Review: 2019-12-22

  • Then: every 6 years

  • End of reporting period: 3 months after required.

Article 7 & 8: Flood Risk Management Plans

Content:

  • See Annex A.

  • First report: 2015-12-22

  • Review: 2021-12-22

  • Then: every 6 years

  • End of reporting period: 3 months after required.

Article 16: Report from the Commission to the EU Parliament

  • First report: 2018-12-22

  • Then: every 6 years
